behavior_in_task_description.reason: test_dayjs_used_for_date_parsing asserts exact literal strings like 'dayjs(item.x[1]).valueOf()' and 'dayjs(item.x[0]).valueOf()' and checks absence of 'new Date(item.x[1] ?? "").getTime()'. These exact code patterns are not stated in instruction.md; the instruction only says to use dayjs instead of Date constructor, without specifying the exact method signatures like .valueOf() vs .getTime() on dayjs objects.

no_solution_leakage.reason: instruction.md explicitly states 'The dayjs library is already imported in this file and should be used for date parsing instead of JavaScript's built-in Date constructor' and describes the exact function (createChartOptions) and the exact nature of the change (date-to-timestamp conversion in scale computation). This effectively tells the agent exactly what to change.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: test_dayjs_used_for_date_parsing checks for exact strings 'dayjs(item.x[1]).valueOf()' and 'dayjs(item.x[0]).valueOf()'. An alternative correct fix like 'dayjs(item.x[1]).toDate().getTime()' or 'dayjs(item.x[1] ?? "").valueOf()' or '+dayjs(item.x[1])' would fail this test despite being functionally correct.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: test_dayjs_used_for_date_parsing is purely a text-matching test (reads file content and checks for string patterns). While other tests (test_vitest_unit_tests_pass, test_date_parsing_produces_valid_numbers, etc.) do execute code, the primary fail-to-pass test is entirely string-based. Not every test is text-only, but the key discriminating f2p test is.

test_not_tautological.reason: test_date_parsing_produces_valid_numbers and test_chart_options_scale_calculation run independent Node scripts that test dayjs behavior in isolation—they don't exercise the actual modified code. They pass regardless of whether the bug fix is applied, since they construct their own logic. The only test that truly detects the fix is test_dayjs_used_for_date_parsing, which is a text match. The vitest test (test_vitest_unit_tests_pass) skips if the test file doesn't exist.

behavior_in_tests.reason: instruction.md lists TypeScript compilation, lint, format, build, and unit tests as verification steps; all of these are tested (test_typescript_compiles, test_repo_lint, test_repo_format, test_repo_build, test_repo_unit_tests).

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named, individually documented with docstrings explaining whether they are fail-to-pass or pass-to-pass, and grouped logically.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task does not produce structured data output like JSON/CSV/API/DB schemas.

no_typos.reason: No typos found in filenames, paths, commands, or variable names in the instruction or test files.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: instruction.md names the exact function (createChartOptions), the exact file path, and describes the exact buggy pattern ('new Date() constructor' for 'x-axis time scale calculations'), essentially pinpointing the exact lines to change.
